flavor.age: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.
flavor.alignment: Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly toward the gentler aspects of chaos. They value and protect others' freedom as well as their own, and they are more often good than not.
flavor.size_description: Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

features.name: Trance
features.desc: Elves do not sleep. Instead they meditate deeply, remaining semi-conscious, for 4 hours a day. The Common word for this meditation is "trance." While meditating, you dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive after years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit a human would from 8 hours of sleep.

features.name: Keen Senses
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

features.name: Cantrip
features.desc: You know one cantrip of your choice from the Wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Elf Weapon Training
features.desc: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

features.name: Extra Language
features.desc: You can read, speak, and write one additional language of your choice.

features.name: Elf Weapon Training
features.desc: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

features.name: Fleet of Foot
features.desc: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

features.name: Mask of the Wild
features.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

features.name: Superior Darkvision
features.desc: Your darkvision has a range of 120 feet, instead of 60.

features.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
features.desc: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of the attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

features.name: Drow Magic
features.desc: You know the Dancing Lights cantrip. At 3rd level, you can cast Faerie Fire once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. At 5th level, you can cast Darkness once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Drow Weapon Training
features.desc: You have proficiency with rapiers, shortswords, and hand crossbows.

flavor.age: Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.
flavor.alignment: Humans tend toward no particular alignment. The best and the worst are found among them.
flavor.size_description: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.

flavor.age: A halfling reaches adulthood at the age of 20 and generally lives into the middle of his or her second century.
flavor.alignment: Most halflings are lawful good. As a rule, they are good-hearted and kind, hate to see others in pain, and have no tolerance for oppression.
flavor.size_description: Halflings average about 3 feet tall and weigh about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

features.name: Lucky
features.desc: When you roll a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

features.name: Brave
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

features.name: Halfling Nimbleness
features.desc: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

features.name: Naturally Stealthy
features.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

features.name: Stout Resilience
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

flavor.age: Gnomes mature at the same rate as humans, and most are expected to settle into adulthood around age 40. They can live 350 to almost 500 years.
flavor.alignment: Gnomes are most often good. Those who tend toward law are sages, engineers, researchers, scholars, investigators, or inventors. Those who tend toward chaos are minstrels, tricksters, wanderers, or fanciful jewelers.
flavor.size_description: Gnomes are between 3 and 4 feet tall and average about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Gnome Cunning
features.desc: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

features.name: Natural Illusionist
features.desc: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Speak with Small Beasts
features.desc: Through sounds and gestures, you can communicate simple ideas with Small or smaller beasts.

features.name: Artificer's Lore
features.desc: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magical, alchemical, or technological items, you can add twice your proficiency bonus instead of any other proficiency bonus that may apply.

features.name: Tinker
features.desc: You have proficiency with tinker's tools. Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp) that functions for 24 hours (unless you spend 1 hour to repair it). You can have up to three active at a time; choose: Clockwork Toy, Fire Starter, or Music Box.

features.name: Superior Darkvision
features.desc: Your darkvision has a radius of 120 feet.

features.name: Stone Camouflage
features.desc: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to hide in rocky terrain.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Gnomish Magic Resistance
features.desc: You have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against spells.

features.name: Gift of the Svirfneblin
features.desc: Starting at 3rd level, you can cast Disguise Self with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast Nondetection with it, without requiring a material component. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

5.notes: No material component when cast with this trait.

features.name: Svirfneblin Camouflage
features.desc: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check, you can make the check with advantage. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

rules.trigger: when you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check

flavor.age: Half-elves mature at the same rate as humans but live slightly longer, often exceeding 180 years.
flavor.alignment: Half-elves share the chaotic bent of their elven heritage. They value both personal freedom and creative expression.
flavor.size_description: Half-elves are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: Thanks to your elven blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can’t put you to sleep.

features.name: Skill Versatility
features.desc: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

features.name: Elf Cantrip
features.desc: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Mask of the Wild
features.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

features.name: Drow Magic
features.desc: You know the Dancing Lights cantrip. At higher levels, you can cast Faerie Fire and Darkness once each per long rest.

flavor.age: Half-orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.
flavor.alignment: Half-orcs inherit a tendency toward chaos from their orc parents and are not strongly inclined toward good.
flavor.size_description: Half-orcs are somewhat larger and bulkier than humans, and they range from 5 to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: Thanks to your orc blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions.

features.name: Menacing
features.desc: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

features.name: Relentless Endurance
features.desc: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can’t use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Savage Attacks
features.desc: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon’s damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

features.name: Fey
features.desc: Your creature type is fey, rather than humanoid.

features.name: Charge
features.desc: If you move at least 30 feet in a straight line and then hit a target with a melee weapon attack on the same turn, you can immediately follow that attack with a bonus action, making one attack with your hooves.

features.name: Hooves
features.desc: You are never unarmed. You are proficient with your hooves, which are a melee weapon that deals 1d4 + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage.

features.name: Equine Build
features.desc: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push or drag. In addition, any climb that requires hands and feet is especially difficult for you because of your equine legs. When you make such a climb, each foot of movement costs you 4 extra feet, instead of the normal 1 extra foot.

actions.name: Hooves (Charge Follow-up)
actions.desc: After Charge conditions are met, you can make one hooves attack as a bonus action.

flavor.age: Kenku mature at about the same rate as humans and typically live under a century.
flavor.alignment: Kenku tend toward chaotic alignments, driven by necessity and survival.
flavor.size_description: Kenku are around 5 feet tall and weigh about 90 pounds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Expert Forgery
features.desc: You can duplicate other creatures’ handwriting and craftwork. You have advantage on any ability checks you make to produce a forgery or duplicate an existing object.

features.name: Mimicry
features.desc: You can mimic sounds you have heard, including voices. A creature that hears the sounds you make can tell they are imitations with a successful Wisdom (Insight) check opposed by your Charisma (Deception) check.

features.name: Kenku Training
features.desc: You gain proficiency in two of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics, Deception, Stealth, and Sleight of Hand.

ability_scores.desc: Increase one ability score by 2 and a different one by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Expert Duplication
features.desc: When you copy writing or craftwork produced by yourself or someone else, you have advantage on any ability checks you make to produce an exact duplicate.

features.name: Kenku Recall
features.desc: Thanks to your supernatural memory, you have proficiency in two skills of your choice. Moreover, when you make an ability check using any skill in which you have proficiency, you can give yourself advantage on the check before rolling the d20. You can give yourself advantage in this way a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Mimicry
features.desc: You can accurately mimic sounds you have heard, including voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations only with a successful Wisdom (Insight) check against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier.

flavor.age: Locathah mature to adulthood by the age of 10 but have been known to live up to 80 years.
flavor.alignment: Most locathah are true neutral or have some aspect of neutrality in their alignment. They tend toward good, coming from a culture where compassion for the downtrodden is a commonality.
flavor.size_description: Locathah stand between 5 and 6 feet tall and average about 150 pounds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Natural Armor
features.desc: You have tough, scaly skin. When you aren’t wearing armor, your AC is 12 + your Dexterity modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield’s benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

features.name: Observant and Athletic
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Athletics and Perception skills.

features.name: Leviathan Will
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, stunned, or put to sleep.

features.name: Limited Amphibiousness
features.desc: You can breathe air and water, but you need to be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid suffocating.

limitations: Must be submerged at least once every 4 hours or begin suffocating.

ability_scores.desc: Increase one ability score by 2 and a different one by 1, or increase three different scores by 1.

flavor.age: Harengons reach adulthood in their late teens and typically live under a century.
flavor.alignment: Curious, quick, and free-spirited. Harengons tend toward good and often lean chaotic.
flavor.size_description: You choose your size when you select this race: Medium (average build) or Small (shorter and lighter).

features.name: Hare-Trigger
features.desc: You can add your proficiency bonus to your initiative rolls.

features.name: Leporine Senses
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

features.name: Lucky Footwork
features.desc: When you fail a Dexterity saving throw after seeing the roll but before the DM determines the outcome, you can use your reaction to roll a d4 and add it to the save, potentially turning it into a success. You can’t use this reaction if your speed is 0.

features.name: Rabbit Hop
features.desc: As a bonus action, you can jump a number of feet equal to five times your proficiency bonus, without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.

actions.name: Lucky Footwork
actions.desc: When you fail a Dexterity saving throw, you can use your reaction to roll a d4 and add it to the save, potentially turning it into a success. You can't use this reaction if your speed is 0.

actions.name: Rabbit Hop
actions.desc: As a bonus action, you can jump a number of feet equal to five times your proficiency bonus, without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.

features.name: Draconic Ancestry
features.desc: You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type.

features.name: Damage Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

custom_origin.note: If your table uses Customizing Your Origin, you may reassign the racial ability score increases to different abilities (usually no stacking on the same ability).

flavor.age: Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.
flavor.alignment: Tieflings might not have an innate tendency toward evil, but many of them end up there. Evil or not, an independent nature inclines many tieflings toward a chaotic alignment.
flavor.size_description: Tieflings are about the same size and build as humans. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: Thanks to your infernal heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Hellish Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to fire damage.

features.name: Infernal Legacy
features.desc: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the hellish rebuke spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the darkness spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Maladomini
features.desc: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the ray of sickness spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the crown of madness spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Dis
features.desc: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the disguise self spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the detect thoughts spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Phlegethos
features.desc: You know the friends cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the charm person spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the suggestion spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Malbolge
features.desc: You know the minor illusion cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the disguise self spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the invisibility spell once as a 2nd-level spell. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Stygia
features.desc: You know the ray of frost cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the armor of agathys spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the darkness spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Minauros
features.desc: You know the mage hand cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast Tenser’s floating disk spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the arcane lock spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Cania
features.desc: You know the mage hand cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast burning hands spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast flame blade spell once as a 3rd-level spell. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Legacy of Avernus
features.desc: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast searing smite spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast branding smite spell once as a 3rd-level spell. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Devil's Tongue
features.desc: You know the vicious mockery cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the charm person spell once as a 2nd-level spell. Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the enthrall spell once. You must finish a long rest to cast these spells again with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

notes: This trait replaces the Hellish Rebuke spell of the Infernal Legacy trait (and similar 3rd-level granted spell in other bloodline legacies, if your table applies it that way).

features.name: Winged
features.desc: You have bat-like wings sprouting from your shoulders. You have a flying speed of 30 feet while you aren’t wearing heavy armor.

flavor.age: Aasimar mature at the same rate as humans but can live up to 160 years.
flavor.alignment: Most aasimar are good, but fallen aasimar can drift toward evil.
flavor.size_description: Aasimar are built like well-proportioned humans. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

features.name: Celestial Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to radiant damage and necrotic damage.

features.name: Light Bearer
features.desc: You know the light cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Radiant Soul
features.desc: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your action to unleash divine energy, causing luminous wings to sprout from your back for 1 minute.

features.name: Radiant Consumption
features.desc: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your action to unleash searing light, causing radiant energy to pour out of you.

features.name: Necrotic Shroud
features.desc: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your action to unleash divine energy in a terrifying form.

flavor.age: Genasi mature at about the same rate as humans and reach adulthood in their late teens. They live somewhat longer than humans do, up to 120 years.
flavor.alignment: Independent and self-reliant, genasi tend toward a neutral alignment.
flavor.size_description: Genasi are as varied as their mortal parents but are generally built like humans, standing anywhere from 5 feet to over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Unending Breath
features.desc: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you’re not incapacitated.

features.name: Mingle with the Wind
features.desc: You can cast the Levitate spell once with this trait, requiring no material components, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

features.name: Earth Walk
features.desc: You can move across difficult terrain made of earth or stone without expending extra movement.

features.name: Merge with Stone
features.desc: You can cast the Pass without Trace spell once with this trait, requiring no material components, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. Your ties to the Elemental Plane of Fire make your darkvision unusual: everything you see in darkness is in a shade of red.

features.name: Fire Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to fire damage.

features.name: Reach to the Blaze
features.desc: You know the Produce Flame cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Burning Hands spell once with this trait as a 1st-level spell, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Swim
features.desc: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

features.name: Acid Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to acid damage.

features.name: Amphibious
features.desc: You can breathe air and water.

features.name: Call to the Wave
features.desc: You know the Shape Water cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Create or Destroy Water spell as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Ability Score Increase
features.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Unending Breath
features.desc: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you’re not incapacitated.

features.name: Lightning Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to lightning damage.

features.name: Mingle with the Wind
features.desc: You know the Shocking Grasp cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Feather Fall spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the Levitate spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast Feather Fall or Levitate with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

features.name: Ability Score Increase
features.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Earth Walk
features.desc: You can move across difficult terrain without expending extra movement if you are using your walking speed on the ground or a floor.

features.name: Merge with Stone
features.desc: You know the Blade Ward cantrip. You can cast it as normal, and you can also cast it as a bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Starting at 5th level, you can cast the Pass Without Trace spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast that spell with this trait, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of 2nd level or higher. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

features.name: Ability Score Increase
features.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Fire Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to fire damage.

features.name: Reach to the Blaze
features.desc: You know the Produce Flame cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Burning Hands spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the Flame Blade spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast Burning Hands or Flame Blade with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

features.name: Ability Score Increase
features.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Acid Resistance
features.desc: You have resistance to acid damage.

features.name: Amphibious
features.desc: You can breathe air and water.

features.name: Call to the Wave
features.desc: You know the Acid Splash cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Create or Destroy Water spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the Water Walk spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast Create or Destroy Water or Water Walk with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

flavor.age: Aarakocra reach maturity by age 3 and rarely live longer than 30 years.
flavor.alignment: Most aarakocra are good and rarely choose sides when it comes to law and chaos.
flavor.size_description: Aarakocra are about 5 feet tall. Their wingspan is typically 20 feet. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Flight
features.desc: You have a flying speed of 50 feet. To use this speed, you can’t be wearing medium or heavy armor.

features.name: Talons
features.desc: You are proficient with your unarmed strikes, which deal 1d4 slashing damage on a hit.

flavor.age: Firbolg reach maturity around age 30 and can live up to 500 years.
flavor.alignment: As people who follow the rhythm of nature, firbolg tend toward neutral alignments. Evil firbolg are rare.
flavor.size_description: Firbolg are between 7 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 240 and 300 pounds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Firbolg Magic
features.desc: You can cast Detect Magic and Disguise Self with this trait. When you use Disguise Self, you can seem up to 3 feet shorter or taller. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a short or long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

1.notes: You can seem up to 3 feet shorter or taller.

features.name: Hidden Step
features.desc: As a bonus action, you can magically turn invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack, make a damage roll, or force someone to make a saving throw. You can use this feature once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest.

rules.duration: until start of next turn or broken

features.name: Powerful Build
features.desc: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

features.name: Speech of Beast and Leaf
features.desc: You have the ability to communicate in a limited manner with beasts and plants. They can understand the meaning of your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return.

flavor.age: Goliaths have lifespans comparable to humans. They enter adulthood in their late teens and usually live less than a century.
flavor.alignment: Goliath society, with its clear roles and tasks, has a strong lawful bent. The goliath sense of fairness, balanced with an emphasis on self-sufficiency and personal accountability, pushes them toward neutrality.
flavor.size_description: Goliaths are between 7 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 280 and 340 pounds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Natural Athlete
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill.

features.name: Stone's Endurance
features.desc: You can focus yourself to occasionally shrug off injury. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total. After you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Powerful Build
features.desc: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

features.name: Mountain Born
features.desc: You have resistance to cold damage. You’re also acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Little Giant
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill, and you count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

features.name: Mountain Born
features.desc: You have resistance to cold damage. You also naturally acclimate to high altitudes, even if you’ve never been to one. This includes elevations above 20,000 feet.

features.name: Stone's Endurance
features.desc: You can supernaturally draw on unyielding stone to shrug off harm. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled and reduce the damage by that total. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

flavor.age: Goblins reach adulthood at age 8 and live up to 60 years.
flavor.alignment: Goblins are typically neutral evil, as they care only for their own needs. A few goblins might tend toward good or neutrality, but only rarely.
flavor.size_description: Goblins are between 3 and 4 feet tall and weigh between 40 and 80 pounds. Your size is Small.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Fury of the Small
features.desc: When you damage a creature with an attack or a spell and the creature's size is larger than yours, you can cause the attack or spell to deal extra damage to the creature. The extra damage equals your level. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

rules.trigger: when you damage a creature with an attack or a spell (target larger than you)

features.name: Nimble Escape
features.desc: You can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Goblinoid
features.desc: You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a goblinoid for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a goblinoid.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself.

features.name: Fury of the Small
features.desc: When you damage a creature with an attack or a spell and the creature’s size is larger than yours, you can cause the attack or spell to deal extra damage to the creature. The extra damage equals your proficiency bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest, and you can use it no more than once per turn.

rules.trigger: when you damage a creature with an attack or a spell (target larger than you)

features.name: Nimble Escape
features.desc: You can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

flavor.age: Purebloods mature at the same rate as humans and have lifespans similar in length to theirs.
flavor.alignment: Purebloods are devoid of emotion and see others as tools to manipulate. They care little for law or chaos and are typically neutral evil.
flavor.size_description: Purebloods match humans in average size and weight. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Innate Spellcasting
features.desc: You know the poison spray cantrip. You can cast animal friendship an unlimited number of times with this trait, but you can target only snakes with it. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast suggestion with this trait. Once you cast it, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

1.notes: Unlimited uses, but you can target only snakes with it.

features.name: Magic Resistance
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

features.name: Poison Immunity
features.desc: You are immune to poison damage and the poisoned condition.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Size
features.desc: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

features.name: Magic Resistance
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against spells.

features.name: Poison Resilience
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the poisoned condition on yourself. You also have resistance to poison damage.

features.name: Serpentine Spellcasting
features.desc: You know the poison spray cantrip. You can cast animal friendship an unlimited number of times with this trait, but you can target only snakes with it. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast suggestion with this trait. Once you cast it, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of 2nd level or higher. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

1.notes: Unlimited uses, but you can target only snakes with it.

3.notes: You can also cast this using spell slots of 2nd level or higher.

flavor.age: Githyanki mature at the same rate as humans and typically live about a century.
flavor.alignment: Githyanki tend toward lawful evil. They are rigidly disciplined and value martial prowess above all.
flavor.size_description: Githyanki are similar in size and build to humans. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Decadent Masteries
features.desc: You gain proficiency with light and medium armor, and with the longsword and greatsword.

features.name: Githyanki Psionics
features.desc: You know the Mage Hand cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast Jump once with this trait. When you reach 5th level, you can cast Misty Step once with this trait. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Psionic Spellcasting
features.desc: You know the Mage Hand cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast Jump with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can cast Misty Step with this trait. Once you cast Jump or Misty Step with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

flavor.age: Githzerai mature at the same rate as humans and typically live about a century.
flavor.alignment: Githzerai tend toward lawful neutral. Their lives are ordered and disciplined, and they value mental fortitude.
flavor.size_description: Githzerai are similar in size and build to humans. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Mental Discipline
features.desc: Your innate psychic defenses grant you advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed and frightened conditions on yourself.

features.name: Githzerai Psionics
features.desc: You know the Mage Hand cantrip, and the hand is invisible when you cast the cantrip with this trait. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Shield spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the Detect Thoughts spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

1.notes: The hand is invisible when you cast the cantrip with this trait.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Psychic Resilience
features.desc: You have resistance to psychic damage.

features.name: Mental Discipline
features.desc: Your innate psychic defenses grant you advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed and frightened conditions on yourself.

features.name: Psionic Spellcasting
features.desc: You know the Mage Hand cantrip, and the hand is invisible when you cast the cantrip with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Shield spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the Detect Thoughts spell with it. Once you cast Shield or Detect Thoughts with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race). None of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait.

1.notes: The hand is invisible when you cast the cantrip with this trait.

flavor.age: Changelings mature slightly faster than humans but live about as long.
flavor.alignment: Changelings tend toward pragmatic alignments, shaped by the roles they adopt and the communities they live in.
flavor.size_description: Changelings are lithe and flexible, able to alter their appearance at will. You are Small or Medium.

features.name: Changeling Instincts
features.desc: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

features.name: Shapechanger
features.desc: As an action, you can change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can make yourself appear as a member of another race, though none of your game statistics change. You can’t duplicate the appearance of a creature you’ve never seen, and you revert to your true form if you die.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Skillful Instincts
features.desc: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

features.name: Flight
features.desc: You have a flying speed of 50 feet. To use this speed, you can’t be wearing medium or heavy armor.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: You can see in dim light within 90 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

features.name: Magic Sight
features.desc: Your keen senses can focus to see the presence of magic. You gain the ability to cast the detect magic spell, but only as a ritual. Your spellcasting ability for this spell is your choice of Intelligence or Wisdom. You can also cast this spell normally with any spell slots you have.

features.name: Nimble Flight
features.desc: When you fall, you can use your reaction to make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 10) to stop falling and fly in place until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Silent Feathers
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Stealth skill.

flavor.age: Dwarves mature at the same rate as humans, but they’re considered young until they reach the age of 50. On average, they live about 350 years.
flavor.alignment: Most dwarves are lawful, believing firmly in the benefits of a well-ordered society. They tend toward good as well, with a strong sense of fair play and a belief that everyone deserves to share in the benefits of a just order.
flavor.size_description: Dwarves stand between 4 and 5 feet tall and average about 150 pounds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Dwarven Resilience
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

features.name: Dwarven Combat Training
features.desc: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.desc: You gain proficiency with the artisan’s tools of your choice: smith’s tools, brewer’s supplies, or mason’s tools.

features.name: Stonecunning
features.desc: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

features.name: Dwarven Toughness
features.desc: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

features.name: Dwarven Armor Training
features.desc: You have proficiency with light and medium armor.

features.name: Superior Darkvision
features.desc: Your darkvision has a radius of 120 feet.

features.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
features.desc: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

features.name: Duergar Magic
features.desc: When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Enlarge/Reduce spell on yourself once with this trait, using only the enlarge option of the spell. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the Invisibility spell on yourself once with this trait. You regain the ability to cast these spells with this trait when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Duergar Resilience
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the poisoned condition on yourself. You also have resistance to poison damage.

features.name: Psionic Fortitude
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself. You also have resistance to psychic damage.

features.name: Duergar Magic
features.desc: Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Enlarge/Reduce spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Starting at 5th level, you can cast the Invisibility spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast Enlarge/Reduce or Invisibility with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

3.notes: No material component when cast with this trait.

5.notes: No material component when cast with this trait.

flavor.age: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.
flavor.size_description: Eladrin range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

features.name: Darkvision
features.desc: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

features.name: Trance
features.desc: Elves do not sleep. Instead they meditate deeply, remaining semi-conscious, for 4 hours a day. The Common word for this meditation is "trance." While meditating, you dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive after years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit a human would from 8 hours of sleep.

features.name: Keen Senses
features.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

features.name: Fey Step
features.desc:
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest. When you reach 3rd level, your Fey Step gains an additional effect based on your season; if the effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. Choose your eladrin's season: autumn, winter, spring, or summer. The effects are as follows:
• Autumn. Immediately after you use your Fey Step, up to two creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 minute, or until you or your companions deal any damage to it.
• Winter. When you use your Fey Step, one creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of you before you teleport must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.
• Spring. When you use your Fey Step, you can touch one willing creature within 5 feet of you. That creature then teleports instead of you, appearing in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you.
• Summer. Immediately after you use your Fey Step, each creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of you takes fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 damage).

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

features.name: Elf Heritage
features.desc: You are also considered an elf for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be an elf.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself.

features.name: Trance
features.desc:
You don’t need to sleep, and magic can’t put you to sleep. You can finish a long rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.
Whenever you finish this trance, you can change your season, and you can gain two proficiencies that you don’t have, each one with a weapon or a tool of your choice selected from the Player’s Handbook. You mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory, and you retain them until you finish your next long rest.

features.name: Fey Step
features.desc:
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
When you reach 3rd level, your Fey Step gains an additional effect based on your season; if the effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (choose when you select this race):
• Autumn. Immediately after you use your Fey Step, up to two creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 minute, or until you or your companions deal any damage to the creatures.
• Winter. When you use your Fey Step, one creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of you before you teleport must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.
• Spring. When you use your Fey Step, you can touch one willing creature within 5 feet of you. That creature then teleports instead of you, appearing in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you.
• Summer. Immediately after you use your Fey Step, each creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of you takes fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

ability_scores.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, and increase a different one by 1, or increase three different scores by 1.

flavor.age: Fairies have a life span of about a century.
flavor.size_description: Fairies are a wee folk. Your size is Small.

features.name: Fairy Magic
features.desc: You know the Druidcraft cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Faerie Fire spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the Enlarge/Reduce spell with this trait. Once you cast Faerie Fire or Enlarge/Reduce with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

3.notes: You can also cast this spell using spell slots of the appropriate level.

5.notes: You can also cast this spell using spell slots of the appropriate level.

features.name: Flight
features.desc: Because of your wings, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You can't use this flying speed if you're wearing medium or heavy armor.

limitations: No flying speed while wearing medium or heavy armor.

ability_scores.desc: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

flavor.age: Ratfolk reach adulthood at 12 and can live up to 50 years.

features.name: Brethren of the Swarm
features.desc: Mice and rats of all types will not show unprovoked hostility towards you unless threatened. Additionally, you can communicate simple ideas with them through squeaking Rattus.

features.name: Keen Senses
features.desc: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that involve hearing or smell.

features.name: Scurry
features.desc: You are able to slip and squeeze through small openings. You have advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) to fit through tight spaces and squeezing through narrow passages doesn’t cost you extra movement.

features.name: Appraiser’s Eye
features.desc: You have advantage on Intelligence (Arcana and Investigation) checks to identify objects and their worth.

features.name: Powerful Build
features.desc: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

features.name: Natural Swimmer
features.desc: You have a swim speed of 30 feet and can hold your breath for 15 minutes.

features.name: Hardy Constitution
features.desc: You have advantage against poisons and diseases.

features.name: Tunnel Crawler
features.desc: You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) to navigate while underground.

features.name: Natural Swimmer
features.desc: You have a swim speed of 30 feet and can hold your breath for 15 minutes.

features.name: Hardy Constitution
features.desc: You have advantage against poisons and diseases.

features.name: River Runner
features.desc: You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) to navigate while on or by lakes, ponds, rivers, or shorelines.

features.name: Forest Touch
features.desc: You know the druidcraft cantrip.

features.name: Stealthy Journeys
features.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

features.name: Woodland Tracking
features.desc: You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to find and gather food, and have advantage on Intelligence (Nature) checks to identify and recall information about beasts, monstrosities, and plants.

ability_scores.desc: Increase one ability score by 2 and a different one by 1, or increase three different scores by 1.

flavor.age: Satyrs mature and age at about the same rate as humans.
flavor.alignment: Satyrs delight in life’s pleasures and tend toward chaotic alignments.
flavor.size_description: Satyrs range from just under 5 feet to over 6 feet tall, with builds similar to humans.

features.name: Ram
features.desc: You can use your head and horns to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

features.name: Magic Resistance
features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against spells.

features.name: Mirthful Leaps
features.desc: Whenever you make a long jump or a high jump, you can roll a d8 and add the number rolled to the number of feet you cover, even when making a standing jump. This extra distance costs movement as usual.

features.name: Satyr Magic
features.desc: Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the Charm Person spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the Suggestion spell with it. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can’t cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
